identity.name: Cryptojacking — Stratum Mining Pool Connection via App-ID (T1496)
identity.summary: A compromised host opens repeated short-lived TCP sessions to a mining pool on a Stratum port and speaks the Stratum JSON-RPC protocol (mining.subscribe / mining.authorize). The CortexSim stratum_tcp_connect EAL plugin reproduces this against an authorised in-lab sinkhole on 10.50.0.42:3333. NGFW App-ID classifies the session as cryptocurrency-mining / stratum, the Enhanced Application Log capt…
identity.description: Resource hijacking (cryptojacking) monetises compromised compute by mining cryptocurrency, almost always against a pool over the Stratum JSON-RPC protocol. Stratum miners connect to a pool over TCP on characteristic ports (3333, 4444, 5555, 14444, 45700, etc.), send a mining.subscribe followed by mining.authorize handshake, then receive mining.notify jobs. The detection surface for Cortex is network: NGFW App-ID identifies the application as cryptocurrency-mining / stratum regardless of port, the Enhanced Application Log records the JSON-RPC method strings, and XSIAM Network Security Analytics flags the behavioural shape of repeated, short-lived TCP sessions to a single mining-pool destination/port. This card detects the activity from panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story using app (App-ID), dst_port, dst_ip and session counts, plus an IOC for the stratum mining.subscribe JSON-RPC method observed in the session payload. A correlation rule fuses the App-ID classification, the JSON-RPC payload IOC and the repeated-session behaviour into one resource-hijacking incident.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1496 — CortexSim stratum_tcp_connect EAL plugin (Stratum JSON-RPC mining.subscribe/authorize to pool port)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-NDR-003 requirements (NGFW inline with App-ID + EAL; in-lab TCP sinkhole reachable on 10.50.0.42:3333).
NGFW forwards Enhanced Application Logs into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story carries app and dst_port).

payload.code:
# step-1: Stand up sinkhole TCP listener inside lab CIDR
nc -zv 10.50.0.42 3333 || echo "[CortexSim] sinkhole reachable check (best-effort)"
# step-2: Launch Stratum JSON-RPC sessions against sinkhole
cat > /tmp/stratum-live.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-003
name: "NDR validation — Stratum App-ID"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - 10.50.0.0/24
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, cryptojacking, stratum, eal]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: stratum_tcp_connect
    params:
      target_host: "10.50.0.42"
      target_port: 3333
      iterations: 5
      sleep_seconds: 15
      wallet: "cortexsim-test-wallet"
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/stratum-live.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Outbound TCP sessions to a mining-pool port classified by App-ID as cryptocurrency-mining / stratum.
Stratum JSON-RPC mining.subscribe method observed in the session payload.
Repeated short-lived TCP sessions to the same mining-pool destination/port.

iocs.context: NGFW EAL — JSON-RPC mining.subscribe payload observed in session. Stratum protocol handshake method string present in the outbound mining-pool session content.

biocs.name: NGFW EAL — repeated short-lived TCP sessions to mining pool port
biocs.description: NGFW EAL — repeated short-lived TCP sessions to mining pool port
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter dst_port in (3333, 4444, 5555, 7777, 14444, 45700)
| filter action = "allow"
| comp count() as session_count, avg(bytes_sent + bytes_received) as avg_bytes by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port
| filter session_count >= 4
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, session_count, avg_bytes

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: sinkhole port 3333 reachable
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_ip = "10.50.0.42" and dst_port = 3333
| comp count() as sessions by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, sessions

xql_queries.name: NGFW App-ID — Stratum/cryptocurrency-mining identified on outbound TCP
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("stratum", "cryptocurrency-mining", "bitcoin", "mining-pool") or dst_port in (3333, 4444, 5555, 14444)
| filter dst_ip = "10.50.0.42"
| comp count() as mining_sessions by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app
| filter mining_sessions >= 1
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app, mining_sessions

correlation_rules.name: Cryptojacking — Stratum Mining Pool Resource Hijacking
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(NGFW EAL — repeated short-lived TCP sessions to mining pool port) OR XQL(NGFW App-ID — Stratum/cryptocurrency-mining identified on outbound TCP) OR IOC(stratum-json-rpc-mining-subscribe)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — Cryptocurrency Mining / Stratum
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Repeated short-lived sessions to a single destination port

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app in ("stratum","cryptocurrency-mining") or dst_port in (3333,4444,5555) | fields _time, src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app
preset = network_story | filter dst_port in (3333,4444,5555) | comp count() by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port
Playbook: block mining-pool destination at NGFW, isolate the mining host, kill the miner process, open Impact DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: Cryptojacking App-ID Detection — Stratum Mining
use_cases.description: Validate that NGFW App-ID + EAL and XSIAM Network Security Analytics detect Stratum JSON-RPC mining-pool connections and repeated short-lived sessions to a mining port.

test_cases.objective: Stratum mining sessions are classified by App-ID and surfaced as cryptojacking.
preconditions: NGFW inline with App-ID + EAL forwarding to XSIAM.
steps: Run SIM-NDR-003 step-02 (5 Stratum JSON-RPC sessions, 15s apart).
Inspect Network Security Analytics / network_story for the source IP.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW App-ID — Stratum/cryptocurrency-mining identified on outbound TCP` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `NGFW EAL — repeated short-lived TCP sessions to mining pool port` matches the source with session_count >= 4.

test_cases.objective: Signals fuse into one resource-hijacking incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-NDR-0003` raises a single cryptojacking incident for the source.

preventive_controls: Block known mining-pool destinations and Stratum App-ID at the egress NGFW.
Apply App-ID least-privilege egress; deny outbound to non-standard high TCP ports from server subnets.
Enforce workload runtime protection to kill unauthorised miner processes (Cortex Cloud / XDR).
detection_engineering: Alert on App-ID = cryptocurrency-mining/stratum regardless of destination port.
Baseline per-host destination-port cardinality; flag repeated short-lived sessions to a single high port.
Match Stratum JSON-RPC method strings (mining.subscribe/mining.authorize) in session content as an IOC.
